3.happening: Create an empty list to hold the version numbers.
3.why: Materialising the versions lets us watch the search space shrink — the real API just answers questions about them.

4.why: Versions are numbered from 1, not 0 — that off-by-one matters later.
4.invariant: v is the next version number to add.

5.why: We need the whole list before the search can begin.

5.happening: All {n} versions are listed — the search can begin.
5.why: The search space is now fully defined.

6.why: Each version is a candidate until we prove otherwise.
6.invariant: versions stays sorted ascending by construction.

7.why: Build the list one version at a time.

8.happening: Version {bad} is bad, which is list index {bad - 1}.
8.why: Versions are 1-based but list indexes are 0-based — converting once avoids confusing every later comparison.
8.invariant: Every index at or after bad_index is a bad version.

9.happening: Put the left edge at index 0.
9.why: Every version starts as a candidate.
9.invariant: The answer is always inside [left, right].

10.happening: Put the right edge at the last index ({n - 1}).
10.why: The whole list is live at the start.
10.invariant: The answer is always inside [left, right].

11.why: It gets a real value on the first probe.
11.invariant: mid always sits inside [left, right] once searching.

12.happening: Start the API call counter at 0.
12.why: The problem asks us to MINIMISE these — this counter is the score.
12.invariant: probes counts every isBadVersion call we make.

13.happening: Declare the holder for the API's answer.
13.why: Each probe stores its result here.
13.invariant: is_bad reflects the most recent probe.

14.why: It is filled once the search has converged.
14.invariant: answer is only meaningful after the loop ends.

15.happening: {right - left + 1} version(s) still in play — keep narrowing.
15.why: While more than one candidate remains, another probe can halve the range.
15.invariant: The first bad version is always inside [left, right].

15.happening: left and right have met — exactly one candidate remains.
15.why: A single-element range needs no further probing; it must be the answer.
15.invariant: left == right, and that index is the first bad version.

16.happening: Probe the midpoint of the live range → index {left + (right - left) / 2 - ((right - left) % 2) / 2}.
16.why: Halving is what makes this O(log n) instead of O(n).

17.happening: Count this API call — probe number {probes + 1}.
17.why: Minimising these calls is the whole point of the problem.
17.invariant: One probe eliminates half the remaining versions.

18.why: Because everything from version {bad} onward is broken, and {versions[mid]} is at or past it.
18.invariant: A bad probe means the answer is at mid or earlier.

18.why: Version {versions[mid]} comes before the first bad version {bad}, so it still works.
18.invariant: A good probe means the answer is strictly after mid.

19.happening: The probe came back bad — discard everything to the RIGHT of mid.
19.why: If mid is bad, nothing after it can be the FIRST bad one.

19.happening: The probe came back good — discard mid and everything to its LEFT.
19.why: If mid is good, the first bad version must come after it.

20.happening: Pull the right edge in to index {mid} — mid stays a candidate.
20.why: mid is bad, but it might be the FIRST bad one, so we must not discard it.
20.invariant: Keeping mid is what makes this find the first bad version rather than just any bad one.

22.happening: Push the left edge past index {mid} to {mid + 1}.
22.why: mid is known good, so it can be excluded outright.
22.invariant: Everything left of the live range is proven good.

23.happening: The range collapsed to index {left} — that's version {versions[left]}.
23.why: One candidate remains, and the invariant guarantees it is the first bad version.
23.invariant: We never discarded a range that could contain the answer.

24.happening: Version {answer} is the first bad version, found in {probes} probe(s).
24.why: Every probe halved the search space, so {n} versions took only a handful of calls.
24.invariant: All versions before {answer} are good; {answer} and everything after are bad.

init.happening: Building the list of versions to search.
init.why: We need a concrete search space before we can halve it.

loop.happening: Narrowing the range of candidate versions.
loop.why: Each pass halves how many versions remain in play.
loop.invariant: The first bad version is always inside [left, right].

check.happening: Branching on what the API said about the midpoint.
check.why: The answer decides which half of the range survives.
check.invariant: Exactly one half is discarded per probe.

update.happening: Probing the API and recording its answer.
update.why: The probe is the only way to learn anything about a version.

move.happening: Moving a search boundary — the discarded half dims out.
move.why: Eliminated versions stay on screen so you can see how much was ruled out.
move.invariant: Dimmed versions are excluded, not deleted.

return.why: The range collapsed to a single surviving candidate.
return.invariant: The answer is the earliest version that fails.
